questions.q: What does the acronym IFFI stand for in the context of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: IFFI stands for the International Film Festival of India, the country's flagship state film festival.

questions.q: In which year was the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) first inaugurated?
questions.explanation: IFFI was inaugurated in 1952, making it one of Asia's oldest film festivals.

questions.q: Since 2004, in which Indian state is the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) held annually?
questions.explanation: Since 2004, IFFI has been held annually in Goa as its permanent venue.

questions.q: What is the top award presented for Best Film at the International Film Festival of India (IFFI)?
questions.explanation: The Golden Peacock (Swarna Mayur) is IFFI's top prize, awarded for Best Film.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is primarily organised by which entity?
questions.explanation: IFFI is organised by the Government of India as the nation's official film festival.

questions.q: The Golden Peacock awarded at the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is also known by which Indian-language name?
questions.explanation: The Golden Peacock is known as Swarna Mayur, mayur being the Hindi word for peacock.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is best described as which type of event?
questions.explanation: IFFI is a film festival, India's flagship window onto world cinema.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is generally recognised as one of the oldest film festivals on which continent?
questions.explanation: IFFI is one of Asia's oldest film festivals, having started in 1952.

questions.q: In which month of the year is the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) typically held in Goa?
questions.explanation: IFFI is typically held in November each year at its Goa venue.

questions.q: Which bird gives its name to the top prize of the International Film Festival of India (IFFI)?
questions.explanation: The peacock gives its name to IFFI's Golden Peacock, India's national bird.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the very first International Film Festival of India (IFFI) held in 1952?
questions.explanation: The first IFFI was held in Bombay (Mumbai) in 1952 before travelling to other cities.

questions.q: Before 2004, the competitive editions of the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) were most associated with which city?
questions.explanation: Before settling permanently in Goa in 2004, IFFI's competitive editions were largely held in New Delhi.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) in Goa is jointly organised with the government of which state?
questions.explanation: IFFI is organised in partnership with the Government of Goa, the host state since 2004.

questions.q: Which Indian government ministry has primary responsibility for organising the International Film Festival of India (IFFI)?
questions.explanation: IFFI is organised under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting through the Directorate of Film Festivals (later NFDC).

questions.q: Until its merger, which government body was chiefly responsible for conducting the International Film Festival of India (IFFI)?
questions.explanation: The Directorate of Film Festivals long conducted IFFI before it was merged into the NFDC.

questions.q: Roughly how many editions of the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) had been completed by 2024?
questions.explanation: The 2024 edition was the 55th IFFI, counting from its 1952 inception.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is internationally accredited by which body that recognises competitive film festivals?
questions.explanation: IFFI is accredited by FIAPF, the international federation of film producers' associations.

questions.q: Which iconic Goa venue is closely associated with hosting the International Film Festival of India (IFFI)?
options: Kala Academy and the INOX complex in Panaji
questions.explanation: IFFI screenings in Goa are centred on venues such as the Kala Academy and INOX in Panaji.

questions.q: At the International Film Festival of India (IFFI), the Golden Peacock for Best Film is decided by which group?
questions.explanation: The Golden Peacock winner is selected by an international jury from the festival's competition section.

questions.q: The 'Indian Panorama' section of the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) primarily showcases which films?
options: The best of Indian cinema for the year
questions.explanation: The Indian Panorama section presents a curated selection of the year's finest Indian films.

questions.q: Which lifetime-achievement style honour is conferred at the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) on distinguished film personalities?
options: The Satyajit Ray Lifetime Achievement Award
questions.explanation: IFFI confers the Satyajit Ray Lifetime Achievement Award on eminent figures of world cinema.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) was made a permanent annual event in Goa from 2004 mainly for what reason?
options: To merge it with a music festival
To give it a fixed, permanent venue
Because Goa was the festival's birthplace
questions.explanation: IFFI shifted to Goa in 2004 to give the festival a fixed, permanent annual venue rather than rotating between cities each year.

questions.q: The very first International Film Festival of India in 1952 was notable for introducing Indian audiences and filmmakers to which film movement?
questions.explanation: The 1952 festival exposed Indian filmmakers to Italian Neorealism, profoundly influencing directors like Satyajit Ray and Bimal Roy.

questions.q: For many years after 1952, the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) alternated between which two formats in different years?
questions.explanation: For years IFFI alternated between a competitive edition and a non-competitive 'Filmotsav' edition held in different cities.

questions.q: The non-competitive editions of the International Film Festival of India that rotated among Indian cities were commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The rotating non-competitive editions were known as 'Filmotsav' before the festival was consolidated in Goa.

questions.q: Which prize at the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) carries the highest cash component awarded to the Best Film winner?
questions.explanation: The Golden Peacock for Best Film carries IFFI's highest cash prize, shared between the director and producer.

questions.q: At the International Film Festival of India (IFFI), the Silver Peacock is typically awarded in which categories?
options: Best Director, Best Actor and Best Actress
questions.explanation: Silver Peacocks at IFFI honour categories such as Best Director and the Best Actor and Best Actress awards.

questions.q: At the International Film Festival of India (IFFI), the Golden Peacock for Best Film is contested within which section of the festival?
questions.explanation: The Golden Peacock is contested in IFFI's International Competition section featuring films from around the world.

questions.q: Which Indian agency took over the conduct of the International Film Festival of India (IFFI) after the Directorate of Film Festivals was subsumed?
options: The Central Board of Film Certification
questions.explanation: After the Directorate of Film Festivals was merged in, the National Film Development Corporation (NFDC) took over conducting IFFI.

questions.q: The International Film Festival of India (IFFI) is often described as India's flagship festival because of which distinctive role?
options: It is the only festival that screens silent films
It serves as India's primary state window onto world cinema
It is run by a private television channel
questions.explanation: IFFI is India's flagship state festival, serving as the nation's principal window onto world cinema.
